title: Logic, Sets, Counting, and Decision Rules · Mastery Check
summary: Audit eligibility rules and resource allocation without double-counting people or contradicting policy. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a tested eligibility and allocation rule set. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent categories, subsets, unions, intersections, and complements from precise definitions.
Use Venn diagrams and inclusion-exclusion to count overlapping groups exactly once.
Distinguish statements, predicates, quantifiers, negation, converse, inverse, and contrapositive.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.instruction: 29 records meet rule A, 22 meet rule B, and 11 meet both. How many meet A or B? Use the result to decide Decision Consistency for a tested eligibility and allocation rule set.
